title: Stelis fortunae
taxonomy: Stelis fortunae is a species within the genus Stelis, which belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The genus Stelis is a group of small to medium-sized orchids, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. Stelis fortunae, like other members of the genus, is a type of epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants, usually trees, in its natural habitat. The classification of Stelis fortunae is further detailed at the species level, where it is identified by its unique characteristics and morphology. The classification of Stelis fortunae reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared traits with other orchids within the Orchidaceae family.

intro: Stelis fortunae, also known as "Fortuna's Stelis," is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis fortunae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis fortunae is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture, to utilize its therapeutic potential.
